rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Sydney A. Luscutoff, And Luscutoff, Lendormy Associates Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony From Plaintiff And Extend Plaintiffs Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 8, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT SYDNEY LUSCUTOFF, LUSCUTOFF, LENDORMY & ASSOCIATES' Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony From Plaintiff And Extend Plaintiffs Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions (part 1 of 2). Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: The Motion is granted as follows: (1) The request to extend the deposition for a further 7 hours is granted. The court finds that the issues are sufficiently complex to warrant the extension of time in view of the answers given and the potential issues at trial. In view of the plaintiff's condition, the deposition will proceed in two sessions of four hours and three hours on separate days, one week apart unless otherwise agreed by counsel. Counsel are to meet and confer on available deposition dates and inform the court of the new dates. During the deposition, the only objections that may be made by deponent's counsel are as to form of the question and privilege. Except for objections as to privilege, the witness must answer the question. As to privilege objections, colloquy by counsel is unnecessary. For the purposes of the deposition, it will be assumed that the witness has been instructed not to answer and counsel for the defendant may bring a further motion to compel deposition testimony concerning such objections. (continued on part 2) =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: MONICA HUJAZI, AN INDIVIDUIAL AND TRUSTEE OF THE VS. SYDNEY A. LUSCUTOFF et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendant Sydney Luscutoff, Luscutoff, Lendormy & Associates' Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony From Plaintiff And Extend Plaintiffs Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions (For Posting Of Tentative Ruling)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 8, 2015, Line 1, Defendant Sydney Luscutoff, Luscutoff, Lendormy & Associates' Motion To Compel Further Deposition Testimony From Plaintiff And Extend Plaintiffs Deposition And Request For Monetary Sanctions (For Posting Of Tentative Ruling) (part 2 of 2). (2) All questions contained in defendant's separate statement, except the fourth question (referenced at transcript 41:25) are ordered answered. An answer to the fourth question is denied based on the form of the question. The objections as to privacy are overruled. The court finds that the questions concerning asset holdings and forms of ownership are sufficiently necessary to overcome the limited privacy privilege as posed. Whether or not the deposition testimony is usable in other proceedings does not restrict the scope of permissible discovery with respect to the relevance of the questions. The court does not find that the challenged questions concerning asset holdings and title are "irrelevant" for discovery purposes. (3) The objections pertaining to the tax return privilege are overruled. The contents of tax returns are privileged under the privacy privilege to a limited extent but not the fact whether or not tax returns were filed. (4) Defendant's objections to statements contained in the declarations are overruled. (5) Sanctions are granted in the amount of $1,050. The court finds that the privacy objections were not substantially justified. No sanctions are awardable for contesting the request for a time extension. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: WILDA L WHITE VS. GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN MD ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Defendants Girish Subramanyan, M.D. And San Francisco Psychiatrists, Inc.S Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Records Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions [Ccp Section 1987.1]
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Friday May 8, 2015, Line 4, DEFENDANT GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN, SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHIATRISTS, INC.'S Motion For Order Directing Compliance With Records Subpoenas And Request For Sanctions [Ccp Section 1987.1]. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Parties are to meet and confer on terms of a suitable protective order and to meet and confer on the scope of the records requests. In re Lifschutz authorizes psychiatric records to the extent that plaintiff has put her psychiatric and mental health condition at issue. The description in Attachment 3 to the subpoenas appears overbroad. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to nrosenquest@earthlink.net with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests.
